C[Dmitry_VL: We have a big time difference. I'm from Vladivostok. That's somewhere near Japan, China, and Korea.
gmckean: I don't mind a time zone difference. Were you at that championship? That's really cool
C[Dmitry_VL: I started playing go with my daughter after the championships

C[Dmitry_VL: Russian schools play a game of dots. It's a simplified go on paper. Once upon a time, I was good at this game. Then one day I was looking through old issues of Nauka i Zhizn magazine - they printed science fiction stories there. In the 1975 magazines, I found a series of articles about go. I thought it was interesting, similar to ""dots"", I should learn it. But at that time there was no one in Vladivostok to teach me. It wasn't until 30 years later, in 2022, when my daughter wanted to learn to play go, that I started playing with her.
